reasoning: The informal statement "ℚ has no proper subgroups of finite index" is equivalent to "every proper additive subgroup of ℚ has infinite index." The Lean signature quantifies over arbitrary `H : AddSubgroup ℚ` with `H ≠ ⊤` (which precisely means H is a proper subgroup) and concludes `H.index = 0`. In Mathlib, `Subgroup.index` / `AddSubgroup.index` is defined as `Nat.card (G ⧸ H)`, which by convention returns `0` exactly when the quotient is infinite. Thus `H.index = 0` is the standard Mathlib encoding of "H has infinite index." The Lean statement therefore expresses exactly the informal claim.
